body:
**Scope:** stand up the new **`custodian` crate** (L4, §5) — the home for the four loops and the durability plane. This slice builds the scaffold every later slice stands on: **single active custodian per zone** leader-elected via the existing `Coordination::elect_leader` (fenced `Leadership` token, `traits/src/lib.rs:209-211`); the reconciliation control-loop skeleton; the **zone-local failure-domain-aware selector** (an opaque domain id per D server + the distinctness invariant), shared by the write fan-out and custodian re-placement; and the **OpenTelemetry seam** wired (`tracing` + `tracing-opentelemetry`, exposing **both** a Prometheus endpoint and OTLP push, no hardcoded backend; ADR-0012), so every later loop emits from its first commit.

**Definition of done**
- [ ] a single active custodian is elected and fenced; a deposed leader's actions are rejected
- [ ] the failure-domain selector places a chunk's `n` fragments across `n` distinct domains where topology allows
- [ ] the OTLP/Prometheus exporter emits a first custodian metric; no backend hardcoded
- [ ] `custodian` depends only on traits/core/proto (ADR-0010), never a concrete backend

**Needs:** M3.1, M3.2. **Refs:** proposal 0005 §"Single active custodian"/§"Failure-domain-aware placement"/§"The durability plane"; ADR-0011/0012; architecture §5, §7.3.

milestone.title: M3 — Custodians
milestone.description: The self-maintaining durability plane (L4): GC, scrub, reconstruction, rebalance + durability telemetry. Makes single-zone trustworthy; gates the Step-2 release. Plan: proposal 0005.

title: M3.3 — custodian crate skeleton: single-active leadership + failure-domain selector + OTel seam
